display.purpose: Page-level headers on the public profile page only. Not used in admin or customer dashboards.

headline.purpose: Section titles, dialog headers, sidebar page name.

title.purpose: Card titles, form section headers, table column group labels.

body.purpose: All prose, table cells, input text, descriptions. Max 65-75ch line length.

label.purpose: Form labels, table headers, badge text, stat card labels.

mono.purpose: NFC slugs, URLs, copy-button targets, code snippets. Signals machine-readable value.

shadows.name: float
shadows.purpose: Modals, dialogs, dropdown menus, tooltips. Floating elements only.

shadows.name: float-strong
shadows.purpose: Full-screen modals and sheets on dark/gradient backgrounds (public profile page).

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions.

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Sidebar collapse, panel slide, dropdown open.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action. Amber Signal background, Charcoal text. Used for the most important action on any given screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: #f59e0b; color: #111827; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; padding: 10px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #d97706; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f59e0b; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #b45309; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action. Transparent, border on hover. Does not compete with the primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: transparent; color: #374151; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; padding: 10px 20px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); outline: none; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #e5e7eb; background: #f3f4f6; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f59e0b; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-ghost:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Irreversible actions: delete, revoke. Rose background, white text. Used only when the action cannot be undone.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; background: #e11d48; color: #ffffff; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; padding: 10px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); outline: none; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background: #be123c; } .ds-btn-destructive:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e11d48; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-destructive:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form input. Flat with subtle border; amber focus ring signals activation without shouting.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-label { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; color: #374151; letter-spacing: 0.01em; } .ds-input { background: #ffffff; color: #111827; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.5; padding: 10px 14px; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 10px; width: 100%; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9ca3af; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #f59e0b; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(245, 158, 11, 0.18); } .ds-input:disabled { background: #f3f4f6; color: #9ca3af; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Content Card
components.description: Standard document-flow card. Distinguished from the page background by a tonal step, not a shadow.
components.html:
NFC Items

Manage your physical NFC cards, stands, and tags.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 600; color: #111827; margin: 0 0 6px 0; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: #6b7280; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Active Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar navigation in active state. Amber Signal background on Charcoal sidebar. The only place amber appears in bulk.
components.html: NFC ItemsCustomers
components.css: .ds-sidebar { background: #111827; padding: 8px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; border-radius: 8px; width: 200px; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #9ca3af; text-decoration: none; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: #f3f4f6; } .ds-nav-active { background: #f59e0b; color: #111827; } .ds-nav-active:hover { background: #d97706; color: #111827; } .ds-nav-icon { width: 16px; height: 16px; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Inline status indicator for NFC item active/inactive state.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-badge-active { background: #fef3c7; color: #d97706; } .ds-badge-inactive { background: #fff1f2; color: #be123c; }

components.name: NFC URL Display
components.description: The primary deliverable after card creation. Slug URL in Geist Mono with one-click copy. The most important element on the card creation screen.
components.html:
NFC URL
https://beemhive.com/t/jane-smith
components.css: .ds-nfc-url { background: #f3f4f6; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 10px; padding: 14px 16px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-nfc-label { font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; color: #6b7280; letter-spacing: 0.01em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-nfc-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; } .ds-nfc-code { font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; color: #111827; background: none; border: none; flex: 1; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-nfc-copy { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 6px 12px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 8px; font-family: Geist, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; color: #374151; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-nfc-copy svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; } .ds-nfc-copy:hover { background: #f9fafb; border-color: #d1d5db; }

narrative.overview:
Beem Hive is a tool for people who move. The admin processes orders and manages customers without ceremony. The customer builds a profile they hand out at conferences, client meetings, first introductions. The interface should operate with the same quiet confidence as the physical card itself: professional, immediate, no friction.

The palette comes directly from the logo: amber, charcoal, white. Three values. No additional hues are introduced unless a function demands it (red for errors, a secondary tonal difference for data viz). Amber carries intentional weight: it is the bee's color, the Hive in the wordmark, and the reason someone reaches for this product over a plain QR sticker. When amber appears on screen, it means something is primary, active, or worth attention. This is not decoration; it is signal.

The system rejects the interchangeable SaaS dashboard aesthetic (blue primary, identical stat cards, no personality), the link-in-bio kitsch of Linktree and its clones, the density and grey monotony of enterprise software, and the gratuitous gradient-and-blob exuberance of startup-y tools. The visual language is restrained, not colorless; professional, not corporate; confident, not loud.
keyCharacteristics: Three-color brand identity: amber, charcoal, white. Errors use rose. No other hues.
Flat tonal layering for depth. No decorative shadows.
Single typeface (Geist) with hierarchy through weight and scale contrast only.
Amber reserved for primary actions and live/active states. Its rarity is the point.
The public /t/[slug] profile page is the product's face to the world; it should feel elevated, not like a free bio link.

rules.name: The Three-Color Rule
rules.body: Brand amber, charcoal, and white account for all intentional color. Rose is functional (errors). Blue and purple appear only in product-type badges as the smallest possible scoped exception. No new hues enter the system without deliberate justification.

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Amber Signal appears on 10% or less of any given screen in the admin and customer dashboards. Its presence means: this is the primary action or the live state. Diluting it by using it everywhere makes it mean nothing.

rules.name: The Weight-Contrast Rule
rules.body: Adjacent typographic elements must differ by at least one full weight step. A flat scale where everything is 400 medium-gray is a symptom of not trusting hierarchy. Contrast is what lets the eye navigate.

rules.name: The Mono Signal Rule
rules.body: Monospace text means 'this is a machine-readable value': a slug, a URL to copy, a code snippet. Never use Geist Mono for decoration or to make something look technical.

rules.name: The Flat-First Rule
rules.body: If it sits in the document flow, it is flat. Shadows are not decoration; they are a statement about position in the z-axis. Reserve them for things that are literally above.

dos: Do use Amber Signal (#f59e0b) for primary buttons, active nav items, and the login brand panel. Nothing else.
Do express depth through tonal layering (Surface Sunken to Surface to Surface Raised). Three steps is enough.
Do lead with Charcoal text on white and white text on Charcoal. These are the two readable combinations at any size.
Do render slugs, NFC URLs, and any machine-readable value in Geist Mono. The typeface signals 'copy this.'
Do give the public /t/[slug] profile page its own elevated visual register. It is not the dashboard; it is the product.
Do maintain weight contrast of at least one full step between adjacent typographic elements.
Do size the NFC URL display as though it is the most important thing on the card-creation screen, because it is.
donts: Don't use a blue primary color. The generic SaaS dashboard look (blue primary, identical stat cards, no visual personality) is the primary anti-reference for this product.
Don't make the public profile look like a Linktree clone: no gradient blobs, no pastel bubbles, no stacked rounded boxes with emoji icons.
Don't build dense, grey, row-heavy admin tables without visual hierarchy. Every screen should have a clear focal point.
Don't use gradients, blob shapes, or emoji-heavy copy in the dashboard. This is a professional tool, not a startup landing page.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Single solid color only; emphasis through weight or size.
Don't add a second accent color because the interface feels flat. Fix the weight and scale, not the palette.
Don't introduce new hues for decorative purposes. The Three-Color Rule holds.
Don't shadow document-flow cards. The flat surface is a feature.
